posts.name: Julian Marshall
posts.cooked:
My son is getting married next year and his fiancée is into wine. She was born in 1975 so I would like to buy a bottle of Bordeaux as a wedding present. I was in short trousers when they were released and it’s not a vintage I’ve ever really got into, so I have little experience.

Apart from First Growths, which wines would you recommend?

posts.name: Christopher Foley
posts.cooked:
Ch. Giscours.
Its the only '75 I have a tasting note for, from last year. Based on my note and one more recent on CT, its easily recommended.

posts.name: Bill Sweeney
posts.cooked: La Mission Haut Brion

posts.name: Ramon Cabrera
posts.cooked: 1975 Magdelaine.

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked:
Leoville las Cases and Pichon Lalande.

WineBerserkers – 27 Apr 17

Marvelous tasting of Leoville las Cases last night

Last night, I attended a wonderful tasting of Leoville las Cases organized by my good friend Panos Kakaviatos and featuring Pierre Graffeuille of Leoville las Cases. The dinner was in the back room at Ripple and featured a fabulous meal prepared by...

WineBerserkers – 20 Oct 17

TN: Pichon Lalande dinner with Nicolas Glumineau

PICHON LALANDE DINNER WITH NICOLAS GLUMINEAU - Taberna del Alabardero, Washington D.C. (10/18/2017) Thanks to Panos, we in DC have been blessed with a number of top Chateau dinners. This time it was Pichon Lalande with Nicolas Glumineau, the general...

posts.name: Paul Jaouen
posts.cooked: La Mish

posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked: Las Cases and LMHB.

posts.name: Jay Miller
posts.cooked: I have no recent experience but I recall Mark G saying that right bank 1975s were generally doing well.

posts.name: Arv R@()
posts.cooked:
Le Gay was good that year, as were many other Pomerols.

It may be easier to find them in your location than stateside at this point.

posts.name: Julian Marshall
posts.cooked: Thanks to all of you for the ideas, I shall start looking!

posts.name: Chris Bublitz
posts.cooked: Pichon Lalande

posts.name: Pat_Martin
posts.cooked:
I had a stellar bottle of the 75 Pichon Lalande recently.

A real 75 sleeper was* long the Haut Bages Liberal, with stupendous depths of iron filings, pulverized schist, and basaltic balance. Quite dry and firm, it was nonetheless a thrilling drink if you go for a medieval sort of beverage. *I’ve not had it in 10 years or so

posts.name: Pat_Martin
posts.cooked: Another under the radar 75 (that I likewise haven’t come across in 10+ years) is the Chateau Lascombes. Correct bottles of the 75 Beychevelle can also be excellent.

posts.name: Jonathan Gr/-\|-|/-\/\/\
posts.cooked: I had an awesome bottle of 75 Beau Sejour Becot last year in a mag.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Evangile and Trotanoy are good choices!

posts.name: yaacovbarselah
posts.cooked:
it’s my year as well, and i’ve had most of the better ones except for petrus. la mish can be insanely great but not cheap. trotonoy is the other one i’d chase. haut brion can be stupendous as well.

i’d not sleep on 1975 mayacamas and heitz btw.

posts.name: M a r k K a p l a n
posts.cooked:
1975 La Tour Haut Brion.

Better yet, 1975 Dom Perignon, but that is the answer to a question that wasn’t asked.
posts.display_username: M a r k K a p l a n

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: '75 Lynch-Bages is really good.

posts.name: J a y H a c k
posts.cooked: I just opened a 75 Cheval Blanc (not technically a first growth) and it was excellent but not outstanding. I have had a lot of them over the years because 1975 was the first vintage of the century after I graduated from law school. The best was Latour. Gruaud Larose was good. Lafite was OK but not great. An ex chateau Lascombes that I had about 10 years ago was one of the worst wines I ever had - my TN said something like, “you know that ziplock bag of mushrooms that got lost at the back of the refrigerator for 6 months.”
posts.display_username: J a y H a c k

posts.name: Brad England
posts.cooked: I’ve always loved La Conseillante.

participants.name: J a y H a c k
